exemplar.title: Designing a Figure Skating Dress that Allows for Inclusivity and Sustainability Within Competitions for Elite Sport

detailedExemplarData.title: Designing a Figure Skating Dress that Allows for Inclusivity and Sustainability Within Competitions for Elite Sport

strengths: Strong information-literacy skills evident in systematic gathering, evaluation, and application of diverse sources
Critical engagement in the COP analysis demonstrates how research effectively informs design decisions
weaknesses: Primary research via Instagram DM lacks the formality and credibility of more professional methods (e.g., email interviews or Zoom sessions)
Section on applied skills is incomplete and does not detail how specific techniques (e.g., rhinestone application) enhanced product quality or met success criteria
feedback.calculationMethod: Mini report holistic evaluation: The application of ATL skills is supported by thorough secondary research and critical analysis, yet the work would be strengthened by more formal primary research methods and a fuller account of how hands-on techniques directly improved the final product.

strengths: Clear articulation of the intended product as an affordable, sustainable figure skating dress that aligns closely with project goals
Comprehensive Gantt chart offering a detailed timeline for key activities
weaknesses: Learning goal lacks measurable success criteria to assess skill mastery
ACCESS FM table is incomplete, omitting Customer, Cost, Environment, and Function with measurable descriptors
âRelevantâ SMART target is truncated and does not fully explain how research supports goals
Plan does not specify required resources or a sequenced set of steps to demonstrate feasibility
feedback.calculationMethod: Mini report holistic evaluation: The planning section benefits from a well-defined product vision and an organized timeline, but it requires more specificity in success criteria, a complete set of design filters, and a clearly resourced, sequenced plan to ensure that goals are both measurable and achievable.

strengths: Detailed cost breakdown clearly demonstrates significant savings compared to market prices
Insightful reflection on personal growth, linking skill development to global SDG objectives
Thoughtful analysis of self-management strategies in balancing project work with personal commitments
Visionary thinking in proposing a low-cost dress enterprise for social impact
weaknesses: Reflection on survey data does not explore whether to expand or refine the target demographic based on the findings
Environmental impact evaluation lacks quantitative detail (e.g., amount of waste diverted through second-hand materials)
feedback.calculationMethod: Mini report holistic evaluation: The reflective section excels in evaluating cost efficiency, personal growth, and strategic vision, but it should deepen its analysis of user-demographic implications and substantiate sustainability claims with quantitative evidence.

annotations.comment: Your survey data indicates appeal beyond the target 14â16 age range. Reflect on whether expanding or refining your demographic focus (e.g., 12â18) would better align with user feedback and project aims.

Your learning goal articulates key sewing skills but lacks measurable success criteria. Specify how you will evaluate mastery of each skill (e.g., complete five flawless invisible seams) to make the goal more specific and assessable.

You clearly define an affordable, sustainable figure skating dress aligned to your learning goals. To strengthen planning, outline required resources and sequence practical steps to demonstrate feasibility and support execution.
Your ACCESS FM table starts well with aesthetics but omits other criteria. Expand it to include Customer, Cost, Environment and Function with clear, measurable descriptors for each.
The âRelevantâ SMART target is truncated and unclear. Fully describe how each research area directly supports your learning and product goals to complete this essential target.
Your Gantt chart provides a comprehensive timeline. To enhance clarity, highlight critical milestones and dependencies, focusing on key tasks that drive your project forward.

You demonstrate strong information-literacy by systematically gathering, evaluating, and applying diverse sources. Your COP analysis shows critical engagement with research that effectively informs your project direction.
The Instagram DM interview yielded valuable insights, but reflecting on a more formal method (e.g., email or Zoom) would strengthen the professionalism and credibility of your primary research.

Your section on application skills is cut off and lacks detailed examples. Fully articulate how specific techniques (e.g., rhinestone application) improved your productâs quality and met your success criteria.

You acknowledge using second-hand materials but lack quantitative detail on environmental impact. Include data or estimates (e.g., kilograms of waste diverted) to strengthen your evaluation of sustainability.
Your detailed cost breakdown clearly demonstrates significant savings compared to market prices. This evidence robustly validates your goal of producing an affordable competition dress.

You effectively reflect on personal growth, linking project accomplishments to your skill development and broader SDG objectives. This insight demonstrates deep self-awareness and a global perspective.

Your reflection on balancing project work with personal commitments shows excellent self-management. Document specific weekly schedules or time blocks to illustrate how this strategy supported your progress.

Your vision to launch a low-cost dress enterprise demonstrates visionary thinking and a commitment to social impact. Consider outlining initial steps or resources needed to transform this idea into a viable venture.
